action.reasoning: Observation: The 15-epoch DINOv2 run (iter 11) achieved only 2.31% Quickdraw accuracy, significantly worse than the 2-epoch run's 4.71%. Analysis: Full fine-tuning at lr=1e-3 caused catastrophic forgetting — source val accuracy dropped from 52.9% (pretrained/EMA-epoch-0) to 30.8%, and target accuracy collapsed. The pretrained backbone features are clearly superior for OOD generalization; aggressive fine-tuning destroys them. Decision: Freeze the DINOv2 backbone entirely and train only the classification head for 10 epochs with a high head LR (1e-2). This preserves the SSL-pretrained features while learning a better head than the 2-epoch run. I will also make Mixup default-off and disable domain-balanced sampling to isolate variables. If this improves over 4.71%, I can then try careful partial unfreezing.
action.command: MASTER_ADDR=127.0.0.1 MASTER_PORT=29500 torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train.py --backbone dinov2 --epochs 10 --batch_size 128 --lr 1e-2 --freeze_backbone 1 --mixup_alpha 0 --balanced_sampling 0 --output_name dinov2_freeze10.pt --ema_decay 0.999
